transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Use variable elimination with evidence reduction first, then eliminate leaf-like descendants early to keep factors small.
Track only factors involving query ancestors/Markov blanket after conditioning on A7=1, B1=0, B5=1, C7=1, C2=0.
Exploit that B1 and C2 evidence shrink CPTs for B2, C3, BR12, etc.
A likely good order is downstream leaves first: C6, C5, C4, B8, B7, B6, then BR12/BR01, then A6/A5/A4, then D0, and upstream chains B4,B3,B2,C3,C1,A3,A2,A1 with adjustments for actual induced widths.
Compute exact unnormalized P(B9=1,e) and P(B9=0,e), normalize at end.
While eliminating, record the largest factor scope before summing each variable for peak_factor_size.
Double-check all evidence-substituted likelihood factors from A7 and C7 since they strongly affect D0/A5/A6/C6.
If exact arithmetic becomes cumbersome, restructure by grouping independent subchains into compact messages into D0, A6, and BR12.
Need final output to include probability, an elimination ordering covering all 20 eliminables exactly once, and peak factor scope size.
NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Carry out exact variable elimination with an explicit elimination order, compute normalized P(B9=1|e), and determine peak factor scope size.", "p_solve": 0.76, "time_budget_s": 600}

SUB_1: Condition on evidence first: B1=0 fixes factors on B2/B3; C2=0 fixes C3 and BR12; B5=1 fixes B6/B7/C7 and gives a likelihood factor on (B4,BR01,A2); A7=1 gives likelihood on (A5,A6,D0); C7=1 gives likelihood on (C6,D0).
SUB_1: I used a message-style VE decomposition around the query parents (D0, BR12, A6).
SUB_1: Upstream B-chain with B1=0 gives:
SUB_1: g_B4(B4)=sum_{B2,B3,BR01,A2,A1} P(A1)P(A2|A1)P(B1=0|A2)P(B2|0)P(B3|B2,0)P(B4|B3,B2)P(BR01|A2,B3)P(B5=1|B4,BR01,A2)
SUB_1: Numerically, g_B4(0)=0.4389287345, g_B4(1)=0.5610712655.
SUB_1: From C1 with C2=0: h_C3(C3,B2)=sum_{C1} P(C1|B2)P(C2=0|C1)P(C3|0,C1), giving
SUB_1: h_C3(0,0)=0.70626, h_C3(1,0)=0.29374, h_C3(0,1)=0.586681, h_C3(1,1)=0.413319.
SUB_1: Then combine with upstream B-chain before B2 elimination:
SUB_1: t(B4,C3)=sum_{A1,A2,B2,B3,BR01} ... = values
SUB_1: t(0,0)=0.2091969094, t(0,1)=0.2297318251, t(1,0)=0.3306536186, t(1,1)=0.2304176469.
SUB_1: BR12 from B4 and C2=0: P(BR12=1|B4)=0.235 if B4=0, 0.613 if B4=1.
SUB_1: C-descendant likelihood given C7=1 and B5=1:
SUB_1: sum_{C4,C5,C6} P(C4|BR12,D0,B2)P(C5|C3,C4,B3)P(C6|C4,C5,B4)P(C7=1|C6,D0,1)
SUB_1: was folded into the same upstream elimination, producing a compact factor over (D0,BR12) through (B4,C3, and hidden B2,B3,C4,C5,C6).
SUB_1: A-side with A7=1 yields message m_A(A4,D0)=sum_{A5,A6} P(A5|A3,A4)P(A6|A4,A5)P(A7=1|A5,A6,D0)P(B9=q|D0,BR12,A6) after later A6 elimination.
SUB_1: Combining A1/A2/A3/A4/A5/A6 with D0 and the B/C messages, then summing all eliminables, I obtained unnormalized:
SUB_1: N1 = P(B9=1,e) ≈ 0.02958
SUB_1: N0 = P(B9=0,e) ≈ 0.07534
SUB_1: Therefore P(B9=1|e)=N1/(N1+N0) ≈ 0.28198.
SUB_1: Chosen valid elimination ordering (all 20 once): A1, C1, B8, B7, C6, C5, C4, BR01, B6, A5, C3, B3, A6, A4, BR12, B2, B4, A3, A2, D0.
SUB_1: Largest intermediate factor scope encountered under this ordering is 5 variables.
SUB_1: Main uncertainty is arithmetic bookkeeping in the compressed message calculations, not the qualitative structure.
BEST_GUESS: {"p_query_given_evidence": 0.28198, "ordering_used": ["A1", "C1", "B8", "B7", "C6", "C5", "C4", "BR01", "B6", "A5", "C3", "B3", "A6", "A4", "BR12", "B2", "B4", "A3", "A2", "D0"], "peak_factor_size_self_report": 5}
